Data.text:
You can’t configure a *job-level* retry policy directly in Orchestrator, but you can control retries via Queues and monitor/alert on failures.

**1. Configure retries via Queues (recommended pattern)**
When your process uses Queues:
- Go to **Queues** > **Add** > **Add** to open **Create Queue**.
- Set:
  - **Auto Retry** = **Yes**
  - **Max # of retries** = desired retry count  
- Save the queue and make sure your workflow processes items from this queue.  
Orchestrator will then automatically retry faulted queue items up to the configured limit.  
[Managing Queues in Orchestrator](https://docs-staging.uipath.com/orchestrator/automation-suite/2021.10/user-guide/managing-queues-in-orchestrator)

**2. Handling faulted jobs**
- Jobs that end in **Faulted** must be restarted manually from **Jobs** (or via your own logic/API).  
[Job States](https://docs-staging.uipath.com/orchestrator/automation-cloud/latest/user-guide/job-states)

**3. Alerting on child job failures**
You can’t target “child jobs” specifically, but you can alert on any faulted jobs:

- Enable email alerts:
  - In **Settings** > **General**, select **Enable Alerts Email** and configure email settings.
  - Ensure users:
    - Have a valid email.
    - Have **View** permission on **Alerts**.
- Orchestrator sends:
  - Email every 10 minutes aggregating **Fatal/Error** alerts.
  - A daily **Alerts Dashboard** email including **Faulted Jobs** count.  

[Setting Up Alert Emails](https://docs-staging.uipath.com/orchestrator/standalone/2022.4/user-guide/setting-up-alert-emails)
